raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.3,
    "R1.2": 1.0,
    "R1.3": 1.0,
    "R1.4": 0.0,
    "R1.5": 0.0,
    "R1.6": 0.3,
    "R1.7": 0.0,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 0.7,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": false,
  "clarification_question": "",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The core task is to verify the logical chain from the presented evidence (internal consistency) to the main conclusion (human alignment).",
    "R1.3": "The central responsibility is to assess whether the reported metrics (inter-judge agreement, variance) are sufficient evidence for the claim about tracking human preferences.",
    "RX.1": "The review must consider whether the abstract adequately discloses the uncertainty in using consistency metrics as a proxy for human alignment.",
    "RX.2": "A critical part of the check is to determine if the conclusion overstates the findings by equating reliability with human-alignment.",
    "RX.3": "The check should confirm that the claims are appropriately confined to the stated scope of automated evaluation for text-to-image generation.",
    "RX.4": "The review should consider potential downstream harms, such as the metric reinforcing the biases of its constituent judge models.",
    "RX.5": "The check must ensure that the components of the proposed method (models, benchmarks) are clearly identified for reproducibility."
  }
}

rationale.R1.2: The core task is to verify the logical chain from the presented evidence (internal consistency) to the main conclusion (human alignment).
rationale.R1.3: The central responsibility is to assess whether the reported metrics (inter-judge agreement, variance) are sufficient evidence for the claim about tracking human preferences.
rationale.RX.1: The review must consider whether the abstract adequately discloses the uncertainty in using consistency metrics as a proxy for human alignment.
rationale.RX.2: A critical part of the check is to determine if the conclusion overstates the findings by equating reliability with human-alignment.
rationale.RX.3: The check should confirm that the claims are appropriately confined to the stated scope of automated evaluation for text-to-image generation.
rationale.RX.4: The review should consider potential downstream harms, such as the metric reinforcing the biases of its constituent judge models.
rationale.RX.5: The check must ensure that the components of the proposed method (models, benchmarks) are clearly identified for reproducibility.
